# REGIME READ — 2026-07-23

## Changes Since Previous Read
- **Stage: 4 → 4 (unchanged).** Held — but for the *opposite* reason to last read. Last read the stage was held because one excellent session is not a trend. This read it is held because the rally **did not extend** and the offsetting risks (FOMC + Big Tech earnings) moved *into* the window.
- **THE DOMINANT FACT OF THIS READ: for the first time in four reads, there is a LIVE TQQQ PRICE — $70.28 (−1.53%, Jul 22, 2026 close).** The single largest limitation of the last three reads is **removed**. And it resolves the most important open question decisively: **TQQQ has RECLAIMED THE 200DMA.** $70.28 vs 200DMA $68.09 = **+3.2% above** — one of the four Stage-3 conditions has **FIRED**, confirmed on a real quote, not an inference.
- **BUT the Jul-21 rip did not extend — the exact neutralizer named last read.** I wrote the read hinged on "whether the VXN decline extends to a second and third session." **It did not.** VXN 26.66 → **26.74, +0.30% — the −6.55% decline stalled dead.** And the tape gave a little back: **NDX −0.54%, Comp −0.57%, SPX −0.14%, DJI −0.01%, Russell −0.92% — all five modestly red**, an orderly consolidation ahead of Big Tech earnings, not deterioration.
- **The VIX is still the one gauge moving the wrong way — and it accelerated.** VIX 17.66 → **17.73 (Jul 22 close) → 18.25 (Jul 23 intraday), rising toward 18.** The VXN-down/VIX-up divergence I flagged last read **has resolved toward VIX** this session: VXN stalled while VIX kept climbing. That is the cautious resolution, not the risk-on one.
- **Brent RE-IGNITED — and last read's celebrated "vindication" partially reversed within one session.** I recorded Brent's −6.45% collapse to 85.14 as vindicating three reads of restraint. **Brent is now 92.91 (Jul 23), and hit $94.07 / briefly topped $95 on Jul 22 (+3.4%) — a one-month high.** The re-ignition sequence I dismissed **came back.** Recording that honestly: the collapse was one session; the restraint was still correct not to *score* it, but the "vindication" framing was premature.
- **Total score: −2 → −2 (unchanged).** Breadth held 0 (a shallow red consolidation after a +1.9% rip is no more "deterioration" than the rip was "recovery" — symmetric discipline). Dealer held −1 (VXN stalled above 25, VIX rose). Macro held −1 (10Y up a **third** session). No lens moved.
- **Triggers from previous read:**
  - **Bullish — TQQQ reclaims 200DMA (~$68):** **✅ FIRED — CONFIRMED ON A LIVE PRICE.** $70.28 > $68.09, +3.2%. Unresolved for three reads; now resolved bullish.
  - **Bullish — VXN closes below 25:** **NOT FIRED — STALLED.** 26.74, +0.30%. The decline halted; still 1.74 above the gate.
  - **Bullish — VIX holds below 16:** **NOT FIRED — moved further against.** 18.25, now 2.25 above the gate and rising.
  - **Bullish — second consecutive advancing NDX + semis:** **NOT FIRED.** NDX −0.54% Jul 22 — the advance did not extend.
  - **Bearish — VXN above 30:** **CLEARED** (26.74).
  - **Bearish — VIX above 20 and holds:** **NOT fired but NEAREST bearish trigger — 18.25 and climbing**, the one gauge trending toward its line.
  - **Bearish — drawdowns re-widen (SPX −5%, DJI −4%, Russell −6%):** **NOT fired.** Modest give-back only (SPX −1.6%, DJI −2.0%, Russell −2.8%).
  - **Bearish — TQQQ breaks 13-wk low (~$57):** **CLEARED — decisively.** $70.28, +23.7% above.
  - **Bearish — HY OAS through 300 bp:** **STILL UNMEASURED. Week 15.**
- **Notable lens flips:** **None.** All seven lenses held. The read changed on *facts inside the lenses* — the 200DMA reclaim (Dealer/structure), the VXN stall, the Brent reversal (Cross-asset internal), FOMC + Big Tech earnings entering the window — not on any score crossing a boundary.

> **Snapshot mix (~1-session spread):** equities + **VXN** + 10Y = **Jul 22 close**; VIX + DXY + Brent + Gold = **Jul 23 intraday.** Same offset as last read, and again it matters: **VXN is a Jul-22 close, VIX is a fresher Jul-23 print** — so VIX's continued rise may be registering pre-FOMC/earnings hedging that VXN's Jul-22 close has not yet fully absorbed.
>
> **Headline #1 — a LIVE TQQQ PRICE, $70.28, and the 200DMA reclaim is CONFIRMED.** After three reads flying blind, the instrument the whole Layer-2 rests on is priced, and it sits +3.2% above its 200DMA.
>
> **Headline #2 — the rally did not extend.** VXN stalled (+0.30%), the tape gave back modestly (all five red), and **VIX rose to 18.25.** The prior read's neutralizer resolved to the cautious side.
>
> **Headline #3 — Brent re-ignited to ~$93, topping $95 intraday**, a one-month high — reversing much of last read's celebrated collapse and restoring the oil risk-premium signal.
>
> **Headline #4 — the wall of event risk arrived.** **Alphabet & Tesla reported Jul 22 after the close** (first Big Tech test), and **FOMC Jul 28–29 is now firmly inside the window.**

## Regime Stage Tracker
- **Current Stage: 4 — Regime-change (risk-off, Nasdaq-concentrated), stabilizing.** Held. The Stage-3 gate requires four conditions: **200DMA reclaim (✅ now MET on a live price)**, VXN below 25 (**1.74 away — not met, and it stalled**), VIX below 16 (**2.25 away and rising — not met**), a second advancing semis-led NDX session (**not met — NDX gave back −0.54%**). **One of four now met, three unmet.** A stage change on one met condition — the day the rally failed to extend and vol ticked back up — would be the exact over-read this sequence has avoided.
- **Stage Anchor:** Jul 16, 2026 — VXN re-spike + semis-led all-red tape (TSMC capex / Alphabet Gemini delay). **Unchanged; session 6 of the episode.**
- **Position:** Stage 4, ~6 sessions in, **stabilized but no longer improving this session.** **Stage-3 re-stabilization 45% (held):** the 200DMA reclaim is a real upgrade, offset by the stalled VXN and rising VIX. **Stage-4 grind 47% (held):** still the marginal favorite — the recovery paused into a wall of event risk. **Stage-5 escalation 8% (held):** every bearish trigger still far away, VIX the only one drifting toward its line.
- **Confidence: Medium (held) — but the composition changed.** The single biggest reason confidence sat at Medium for three reads — **no TQQQ price — is now RESOLVED.** That argues for an upgrade. It is offset, one-for-one, by **fresh event risk that did not exist last read: GOOGL/TSLA results dropped Jul 22 after close (gap risk into Jul 23) and FOMC lands Jul 29.** Net Medium: the structural blind spot closed, an event blind spot opened. Still-missing: HY OAS (Week 15), semis-specific leadership, breadth internals.
- **Bullish trigger (→ Stage 3):** VXN closes below 25 (26.74 — 1.74 away, but it **stalled**) AND VIX back below 16 (18.25 — moving away) AND a clean, non-hawkish FOMC that does not re-expand vol — **TQQQ holding above the 200DMA ($68) through the event.**
- **Bearish trigger (→ Stage 5):** **VIX pushes through 20 and holds — the nearest bearish channel, 18.25 and climbing** OR VXN reverses back above 30 OR a hawkish-surprise FOMC / disappointing Big Tech guidance re-widens the drawdowns (SPX −5%, DJI −4%, Russell −6%) OR **TQQQ loses the 200DMA back below ~$68** OR HY OAS confirmed through 300 bp (unmeasured Week 15).

## Regime Verdict
US risk environment **holds Stage 4 (regime-change, stabilizing) with Medium confidence.** The defining event of this read is informational, not directional: **a live TQQQ price ($70.28) after three blind reads confirms the 200DMA reclaim (+3.2%) — one of four Stage-3 conditions FIRED.** But the Jul-21 rip **did not extend** — VXN stalled (+0.30%, still above 25), the tape gave back modestly across all five indices, **VIX rose to 18.25**, and the VXN-down/VIX-up divergence resolved toward VIX. **Brent re-ignited to ~$93 (topping $95 intraday), reversing much of last read's celebrated collapse.** Total holds **−2**; no lens crossed a boundary. Confidence stays **Medium** because the structural blind spot (no price) closed exactly as a new event blind spot opened: **Alphabet & Tesla reported after Jul-22's close and FOMC Jul 28–29 is now inside the window.** The recovery is real and the 200DMA reclaim is a genuine milestone — but it paused into a stacked wall of event risk, which is why the stage is held and not advanced.

## Diverging Signals
- **THE DIVERGENCE RESOLVED — toward the cautious side.** Last read VXN collapsed while VIX rose, and I could not cleanly separate a timing artifact from genuine dispersion. **This session VXN stalled (+0.30%) while VIX kept rising (to 18.25).** The two gauges now point the *same* direction — up — which retroactively argues the Jul-21 VXN plunge was closer to a one-session overshoot than a durable turn. **The vol complex is no longer draining; it is firming into the events.**
- **The strongest signal and the weakest are in the same instrument.** TQQQ **reclaimed its 200DMA (bullish, confirmed on a live price)** — but did so on a day it fell **−1.53%** and its RSI (if the retrieved ~69 is real) sits near overbought after a two-day rip. **A near-overbought reading 8% below the 50DMA is internally inconsistent** (see caveat below) — the momentum picture is genuinely ambiguous.
- **A prior "vindication" is recorded as premature.** Last read I framed Brent's −6.45% collapse as vindicating three reads of restraint. **Brent round-tripped to ~$95 in one session.** The restraint (not *scoring* the earlier rallies) was still correct; the *vindication framing* was not — and saying so is more useful than quietly moving on.
- **Repaired, then partially un-repaired.** Every index gave back a slice of Jul-21's drawdown repair: NDX −5.2%→−5.7%, Comp −5.0%→−5.5%, Russell −1.9%→−2.8%. One session of recovery, one shallow session of give-back — the structural hole (NDX −5.7% below its high) is intact.
- **Macro is quietly the persistent drag.** **10Y rose a third straight session** (4.598 → 4.628 → 4.657) into an escalating tariff docket. A market stabilizing against rising yields and a widening tariff list is not a clean risk-on setup.
- **Contained vs spreading — still blind, Week 15.** HY OAS would tell whether this is a genuine turn or a relief pause inside an intact risk-off regime. Fifteen weeks without it, now into an FOMC.

RSI is flagged uncertain (see below); IV/Greeks/put-call remain not reliably searchable and are marked estimate/unavailable per hard rules.

## TQQQ Read
- **Momentum: genuinely ambiguous, and I will not force it.** Two strong up sessions lifted TQQQ off oversold, then Jul 22 gave back −1.53%. One RSI source reads ~69 (near-overbought), **but a 69 print with price 8% below the 50DMA is internally inconsistent** — the honest read is RSI in the ~50–60 zone, lifting out of oversold toward neutral, with the mean-reversion tailwind that made the oversold condition distinctive now largely spent.
- **Market structure: SIDEWAYS — a genuine change from the prior reads' DOWNTURN, and it is flagged as a change.** With a live price I can apply the definition to a *real* recent 4-week window rather than the stale $88.09/Jun-3 anchor the last reads carried. Recent window ≈ $57 low to ~$76 high: $70.28 is **~−8% below the ~$76 4-wk high** (NOT past −20% → not DOWNTURN, even though VXN ≥ 25) and **~+23% above the ~$57 4-wk low** (NOT past +30% → not RALLY). **→ SIDEWAYS.** Note: on the carried $88.09 the −20.2% marginally clears DOWNTURN, but $88.09 is the *52-week* high (Jun 3), not a valid 4-week high on Jul 22 — so SIDEWAYS is the correct current classification.
- **Trend: below the 50DMA, above the 200DMA — the 200DMA reclaim is the headline.** ~8% under the 50DMA ($76.39); **+3.2% above the 200DMA ($68.09)**, confirmed on a live quote. Holding this shelf through FOMC is the pivotal technical question.
- **Volatility & premium environment: rich but no longer draining.** VXN **26.74, +0.30% — the drain stalled**; est. TQQQ IV **~80%**; EM **±11.3%.** The characterization shifts again: three reads of "premium expanding," one of "draining," now **"holding rich — the drain paused into the events."** IV ~80% and a ±11.3% weekly move remain far outside normal, and vol is firming, not falling, into FOMC + Big Tech earnings.
- **Put-selling context: CAUTIOUS (held).** Constructive: **TQQQ reclaimed the 200DMA on a real price (+3.2%)**, bearish escalation triggers remain far away, premium is still rich (~80% est. IV). Against upgrading: (a) **the rally stalled** — VXN flat above 25, VIX rose to 18.25; (b) **a stacked wall of event risk inside the window — Alphabet & Tesla reported Jul 22 after close (gap risk into Jul 23) and FOMC lands Jul 29**; (c) **Brent re-ignited to ~$95**, restoring an inflation/risk-premium signal; (d) 10Y up a third session; (e) HY OAS unmeasured, Week 15; (f) RSI ambiguous. **Net: CAUTIOUS — the structural picture improved (a real price, 200DMA reclaimed) but the environment is firming vol into a Fed decision and the first Big Tech earnings test, which caps the read below FAVOURABLE.** Context only — no strikes, sizes, expiries, or trade calls.

## Forward Levels & Triggers
- **Bullish trigger (→ Stage 3 / stabilization):** **VXN closes below 25** (26.74 — 1.74 away, but stalled) AND **VIX back below 16** (18.25 — moving away) AND **a clean, non-hawkish FOMC that does not re-expand vol**, with **TQQQ holding above the 200DMA (~$68) through the event.**
- **Bearish trigger (→ Stage 5):** **VIX pushes through 20 and holds — the nearest bearish channel at 18.25 and climbing** OR **VXN reverses above 30** OR **a hawkish-surprise FOMC or weak Big Tech guidance re-widens the drawdowns** (SPX −5%, DJI −4%, Russell −6%) OR **TQQQ loses the 200DMA back below ~$68** OR HY OAS confirmed through 300 bp (unmeasured Week 15).
- **Neutralizer:** **the FOMC Jul 28–29 (decision + presser Jul 29, 2 pm ET) and the Big Tech earnings cluster (GOOGL/TSLA already out Jul 22 after close; more Jul 27–31)** — either can reprice the entire read in one session. Vol is already firming *into* both, which is the asymmetry that matters.

## This Period's Catalysts
- **Jul 22, 2026 (after close):** **Alphabet & Tesla Q2 earnings** — the first major Big Tech test; results drive Jul-23 gap risk ([TheStreet Jul 22](https://www.thestreet.com/stock-market-today/stock-market-today-dow-jones-sp-500-nasdaq-updates-july-22-2026)).
- **Jul 27–31, 2026:** **Remaining Mag-7 earnings cluster** — peak single-name concentration risk for NDX/TQQQ.
- **Jul 28–29, 2026:** **FOMC decision** (statement + press conference Jul 29, 2:00 pm ET; no dot plot this meeting; chair Kevin Warsh) — **the critical scheduled event, inside the window** ([Fed calendar](https://www.federalreserve.gov/monetarypolicy/fomccalendars.htm)).
- **Jul 30, 2026:** Q2 GDP Advance, 8:30 am ET — just outside the window.
- **Ongoing, unscheduled:** whether VXN takes out 25 or firms; whether Brent's re-ignition above $95 extends; further tariff enactment.

## Expiry-Window Flag
- **Catalysts inside the next 5 trading days (Jul 23, 24, 27, 28, 29):** **FOMC decision Jul 28–29** (presser Jul 29) and the **bulk of the Mag-7 earnings cluster (week of Jul 27–31)** — plus the immediate aftermath of **Alphabet/Tesla (Jul 22 after close).**
- **Window: EVENT-WINDOW** — a scheduled central-bank decision *and* mega-cap earnings fall inside the next 5 sessions. Event and single-name risk are **stacked**.

## Structural Calendar Flag (deterministic — always report)
- **Quad-witching:** Last **Jun 19, 2026** (digested). Next **Sep 18, 2026** — **~39 trading days out. OUTSIDE the 2-session window.**
- **Quarter-end rebalance:** Last **Jun 30, 2026** (digested). Next **Sep 30, 2026** — **~47 trading days out. OUTSIDE the 2-session window.**
- **Net:** No structural calendar items inside the 2-session window. Intraday volume/pinning is **not** structurally elevated by calendar mechanics. The stalled VXN, the modest all-red consolidation, the Brent re-ignition, and the rising VIX are genuine directional/event signals — not expiry or rebalancing artifacts. Descriptive only — no trade calls.

## EVENT ALERT — Historical Playbook

- **FOMC, Jul 28–29, 2026 (decision + presser Jul 29, 2:00 pm ET; no SEP/dot plot; chair Kevin Warsh).** Retrievable named history for a comparable setup — an FOMC arriving with vol *firming* off a two-day relief bounce, inside an unresolved risk-off episode, under a new hawkish-reputation chair — is limited, and I will not manufacture averages. Named episodes that bracket the outcomes: **FOMC Sep 21, 2022** — SPX −1.7% on the day, continued lower several sessions, vol *rose* through and after the event rather than crushing. **FOMC Dec 14, 2022** — SPX ~−2.5% on the decision as a hawkish message overrode a soft CPI two days prior, showing a pre-meeting relief rally offers no protection through a hawkish surprise. **FOMC Aug 2024 (post-yen-carry regime-change)** — the closest structural analogue: a vol spike had largely drained into the meeting, the Fed did not re-spike it, and the risk-off episode ended. **These families genuinely diverge; that divergence is the finding.**
- **On vol behavior:** the usual crush-into-a-decision pattern **fails precisely when the Fed surprises hawkish** (the 2022 cases). Current detail that matters: **VXN already gave back much of its episode spike, then STALLED (+0.30%) — and VIX is rising into the meeting (18.25).** Pre-event vol that has already drained *and is now re-firming* has little crush left to deliver and more room to re-expand on a surprise — an asymmetry sharpened by the **new-chair uncertainty** and the **Big Tech earnings overlapping the same week.**
- **Net alert:** the record for this event type in a comparable regime is **two-sided and I will not resolve it into one expectation.** Aug-2024 says an already-drained, firming-but-not-spiking vol episode can be *ended* by an uneventful Fed; Sep/Dec-2022 says a hawkish surprise can re-expand vol and drive multi-session downside regardless of a pre-meeting bounce. **Specific to now:** the decision lands on the **final session of the window**, **VIX is rising into it (18.25)**, **VXN's drain has stalled above 25**, a **new chair (Warsh)** adds message uncertainty, and **Mag-7 earnings stack index- and single-name event risk in the same week.** **Gap risk through Jul 23 (GOOGL/TSLA reaction) and Jul 29 (FOMC) is materially higher than the recent calm suggests, and the asymmetry runs toward vol re-expansion rather than further crush.** Descriptive only — no trade calls.

> **Hard-rules compliance notes:**
> - **Analysis only.** No structures, strikes, expiries, or sizing anywhere.
